sections.label: Section 2.1.257
sections.translation: I wish it was so , indeed .

sections.label: Section 2.1.257b
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.257c
sections.translation: Do you ask me , Phaedria ? You people have cooked up a fine marriage in my absence .

sections.label: Section 2.1.259
sections.original: Eho , an id succenses nunc illi ?
sections.translation: What now , are you angry with him for that ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.259b
sections.translation: What a clever contriver !

sections.label: Section 2.1.260
sections.original: Egone illi non succenseam ? Ipsum gestio
sections.translation: Have I not reason to be angry with him ? I long for him to come into my sight , that he may know that through his faultiness , from being a mild father , I am become a most severe one .

sections.label: Section 2.1.263
sections.original: Atqui nihil fecit , patrue , quod succenseas .
sections.translation: But he has done nothing , uncle , for which you should blame him .

sections.label: Section 2.1.264
sections.original: Ecce autem similia omnia ; omnes congruunt ;
sections.translation: Now , do look at that ; all alike ; all hanging together ;

sections.label: Section 2.1.265
sections.translation: when you know one , you know all .

sections.label: Section 2.1.265b
sections.translation: That is not the case .

sections.label: Section 2.1.266
sections.original: Hic in noxa est : ille ad defendendam causam adest .
sections.translation: When the one is in fault , the other is at hand to defend him ; when it is the other , then he is ready ; they just help one another by turns .

sections.label: Section 2.1.268
sections.original: Probe horum facta imprudens depinxit senex .
sections.translation: The old man , without . knowing it , has exactly described their proceedings .

sections.label: Section 2.1.269
sections.original: Nam ni haec ita essent , cum illo haud stares , Phaedria .
sections.translation: For if it had not been so , you would not , Phaedria , have stood up for him .

sections.label: Section 2.1.270
sections.original: Si est , patrue , culpam ut Antipho in se admiserit ,
sections.translation: If , uncle ; it is the fact , that Antipho has been guilty of any fault , in consequence of which he has been too regardless of his interest or his reputation , I would not allege any reason why he should not suffer what he deserves . But if some one by chance , relying upon his own artfulness , has laid a snare for our youthful age ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.275
sections.original: Ac vicit ; nostrane ea culpa est an iudicum ,
sections.translation: and has succeeded , is it our fault or that of the judges , who often , through envy , take away from the rich , or , through compassion , award to the poor ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.278
sections.original: Ni nossem causam , crederem vera hunc loqui .
sections.translation: Unless I knew the case , I could fancy he was saying the truth .

sections.label: Section 2.1.279
sections.original: An quisquam iudex est qui possit noscere
sections.translation: Is there any judge who can possibly know

sections.label: Section 2.1.280
sections.original: Tua iusta , ubi tute verbum non respondeas ,
sections.translation: your rights , when you yourself don ’ t answer a word — as he has done ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.281b
sections.translation: He acted the part of an ingenuous young man ; after they had come before the judges , he was not able to say what he had intended , so much did his modesty confuse him there through his bashfulness .

sections.label: Section 2.1.285
sections.original: Laudo hunc ; sed cesso adire quamprimum senem ?
sections.translation: I commend him : but why do I hesitate at once to accost the old man ? ( Going forward to DEMIPHO .) Master , welcome to you ! I ’ m glad to see you safe returned .

sections.label: Section 2.1.286b
sections.translation: Ah , excellent guardian ! save you , stay of my family , no doubt , to whom , at my departure , I intrusted my son .

sections.label: Section 2.1.289
sections.original: Iam dudum te omnes nos accusare audio
sections.translation: For some minutes past I ’ ve heard you accusing all of us

sections.label: Section 2.1.290
sections.original: Immerito , et me horunc omnium immeritissimo .
sections.translation: undeservedly ; and me the most undeservedly of them all ; for what would you have had me do for you in this affair ? The laws do not allow a person who is a slave to plead ; nor is there any giving evidence [*] on his part .

sections.label: Section 2.1.293b
sections.translation: I grant all that : I admit this too — the young man , unused to courts , was bashful ; I allow it :

sections.label: Section 2.1.295
sections.original: Tu servus : verum , si cognata est maxime ,
sections.translation: you , too , are a slave : still , if she was ever so near a relative , it was not necessary for him to marry her , but as the law enjoins , you might have given her a portion ; [*] she could have looked out for another husband . Why , then , in preference , did he bring a pauper home ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.299
sections.translation: No particular reason ; but he hadn ’ t the money .

sections.label: Section 2.1.299b
sections.translation: He might have borrowed it

sections.label: Section 2.1.300
sections.translation: from some person or other .

sections.label: Section 2.1.300b
sections.translation: From some person or other ? Nothing more easily said .

sections.label: Section 2.1.301
sections.original: Postremo , si nullo alio pacto , foenore .
sections.translation: After all , if on no other terms , on interest .

sections.label: Section 2.1.302
sections.original: Hui , dixti pulchre : siquidem quisquam crederet
sections.translation: Aye , aye , fine talking ; as if any one would have trusted him , while you were living . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.1.303b
sections.original: Non , non sic futurum est : non potest .
sections.translation: No , it shall not be so ; it must not be . Ought I to allow her to remain with him as his wife a single day ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.305
sections.original: Nihil suave meritum est . Hominem commonstrarier
sections.translation: She merits no indulgence . I should like this fellow to be pointed out to me , or to be shown where he lives .

sections.label: Section 2.1.307
sections.translation: Phormio , do you mean ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.307b
sections.translation: That fellow , the woman ’ s next friend ? [*]

sections.label: Section 2.1.308
sections.translation: I ’ ll have him here immediately .

sections.label: Section 2.1.308b
sections.translation: Where is Antipho at present ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.308c
sections.translation: Away from home .

sections.label: Section 2.1.309
sections.original: Abi , Phaedria : eum require atque adduce huc .
sections.translation: Go , Phedria , look for him , and bring him here .

sections.label: Section 2.1.309b
sections.translation: I ’ Il go straightway to the place .

sections.label: Section 2.1.310b
sections.translation: To Pamphila , you mean .

sections.label: Section 2.1.311
sections.original: At ego Deos Penates hinc salutatum domum
sections.translation: I ’ ll just step home to salute the house-hold Gods . [*] From there , I ’ ll go to the Forum , and summon some of my friends to give me their assistance in this affair ; so that I may not be unprepared , when Phormio comes .

sections.label: Section 2.2.315
sections.original: ITANE patris ais conspectum veritum hinc abisse ?
sections.translation: And so you say [*] that , dreading his father ’ s presence , he has taken himself off ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.315b
sections.translation: Exactly so .

sections.label: Section 2.2.316
sections.translation: That Phanium is left alone ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.316b
sections.translation: Just so .

sections.label: Section 2.2.316c
sections.translation: And that the old man is in a rage ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.317
sections.translation: Extremely so .

sections.label: Section 2.2.317b
sections.original: Ad te summa solum , Phormio , rerum redit .
sections.translation: The whole business , Phormio , rests on yourself alone ; you yourself have hashed it up ; [*] it must all be swallowed by yourself , so set about it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.319
sections.translation: I entreat you —

sections.label: Section 2.2.319b
sections.translation: If he inquires .

sections.label: Section 2.2.319c
sections.translation: In you is all our hope .

sections.label: Section 2.2.319d
sections.translation: Look at this , now :—

sections.label: Section 2.2.320
sections.translation: What if he sends her back ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.320b
sections.translation: It was you that urged us .

sections.label: Section 2.2.320c
sections.translation: I think that will do .

sections.label: Section 2.2.320d
sections.translation: Do help us .

sections.label: Section 2.2.321
sections.original: Cedo senem : iam instructa sunt mihi in corde consilia omnia .
sections.translation: Let the old gentleman come ; all my plans are now ready prepared in my mind .

sections.label: Section 2.2.322
sections.translation: What will you do ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.322b
sections.original: Quid vis nisi uti maneat Phanium , atque ex crimine hoc
sections.translation: What would you have me ? But that Phanium may continue with him , and that I may clear Antipho from this charge , and turn upon myself [*] all the wrath of the old gentleman ?
